new_classes.definition_en: A sparkling wine produced in the French province of Champagne from grapes exclusively harvested in that province.
A quality borne by a wine or grape indicating that it originates exclusively from the French province of Champagne.
A grape wine production process using grapes exclusively harvested in the French province of Champagne, yielding a sparkling wine classified as Champagne.

coverage_explanation: The three primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) Champagne is a sparkling wine — modelled via subClassOf SparklingWine; (2) produced in the French province of Champagne — modelled via ChampagneGrapeProduction (a subclass of GrapeWineProduction) bearing ChampagneRegionOriginQuality; (3) grapes exclusively harvested in that province — modelled via the same ChampagneRegionOriginQuality quality on the production process. A minor gap (score -0.1) is that the concept of "exclusively" cannot be formally enforced in OWL without a universal restriction that would risk over-constraining open-world semantics; the existential someValuesFrom adequately captures the necessary condition but not the sufficiency of exclusivity. No regulatory entity individuals (footnotes, measure types, certificates) specific to this code appear in the running TBox, so none are referenced.
